summary: The suite covers all four core requirements (ProcessInfo model, read_processes with injectable provider, filter_by_threshold, top_n_consumers, generate_alert_report) and explicitly validates the mockability requirement via provider injection. Test classes are logically organized, the module-level MOCK_PROCESSES fixture is well-constructed with diverse values, and tests are independent with descriptive names. Weaknesses: (1) no boundary test for values exactly at threshold (the implementation uses strict >, so cpu_percent==threshold should NOT appear in results — this is never verified); (2) top_n_consumers only tests n=0 as invalid, not negative n; (3) no test for generate_alert_report when only one threshold is set (e.g., memory_threshold=None) to verify reason strings are omitted correctly; (4) patch and MagicMock are imported but never used, suggesting a removed test path; (5) no test that the provider's return value is passed through unchanged (identity check). These gaps leave meaningful room for boundary bugs to slip through, but the happy-path and primary error-path coverage is solid.
